differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic pyrexia syndrome
Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) secondary to BRAF/MEK inhibition
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) secondary to drug-induced systemic inflammatory response
Anaplasmosis (due to geographic location and clinical presentation)
Acute interstitial nephritis (AIN) secondary to targeted therapy
Occult occult metastatic melanoma progression (paraneoplastic or hepatic metastasis)
next_steps: Permanent discontinuation of dabrafenib and trametinib
Ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, and soluble IL-2 receptor (sCD25) levels to evaluate for HLH
Peripheral blood smear for morulae and review of blood cell morphology
Serologic and PCR testing for Anaplasma phagocytophilum and Ehrlichia chaffeensis
Renal ultrasound to evaluate for AIN and monitor creatinine recovery
Liver-specific laboratory workup including viral hepatitis panel and autoimmune markers (ANA, ASMA, IgG)
Systemic corticosteroid therapy if symptoms persist or if HLH criteria are met
